How amphetamine use may be affecting our waterways How amphetamine use may be affecting our waterways How amphetamine use may be affecting our waterways How amphetamine use may be affecting our waterways How amphetamine use may be affecting our waterways The use of amphetamines, a class of stimulant drugs, has risen sharply in recent years, and this trend has a surprising and often overlooked impact on our waterways. When individuals consume amphetamines, a portion of the drug and its metabolites are excreted through urine, which eventually makes its way into sewage systems and, ultimately, into local waterways. Conventional wastewater treatment plants are not designed to fully remove these contaminants, leading to a continuous release of amphetamines and their metabolites into rivers, lakes, and coastal waters.  The presence of amphetamines in water can have detrimental effects on aquatic ecosystems.
